content:
# Asphaltene Stability Index

**Summary**: A metric to assess the propensity of asphaltenes to precipitate in a given oil mixture.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023]] evaluated several experimental tests including the stability of asphaltene (CII & CSI).

- [[2023-al-hakami|Al-Hakami, 2023]] calculated CII to assess the risk of asphaltene precipitation when adding chemicals.

## Scientific Context

- [[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023]] Used to predict the stability of asphaltene based on its chemical composition.

- [[2023-al-hakami|Al-Hakami, 2023]] calculated CII to assess the risk of asphaltene precipitation when adding chemicals.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023]] Calculated from SARA data.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The most unstable asphaltene fraction was observed to have higher heteroatoms. ([[2023-suleiman|Suleiman, 2023, p. 68]])


- The optimal concentration for avoiding asphaltene precipitation was 2.5 wt.% of BL (CII = 0.389). ([[2023-al-hakami|Al-Hakami, 2023, p. 103]])
